text:
A few years ago, my friend Susan told me that every time she passed an old church bell tower—

"Ooh! Look! A giant owl!

Did you hear that?'

A honky-doodle dove...

Oooh! A raccoon?!

A big old owl!

Oww!

That?

Just some owls."

Sometimes, when we're quiet together,
those owls would whistle those old hymns.

One day, little owl had wings.

One day...

Oh look!

A giant sun!

Oo!

Ow!

A birthday parade!

O.

Oh?

That?

Just you!

Go blow your owlish nose!

A birthday party!

Ooh! A ghost!

A kite?!

That?

A big old owl!

O!

That?

A giant mouse!

A mouse!

Oh!

A cookie!

That?

Just you!

A story!

A big old tale.

Just you!


A wizard!

Of course!

Owwww!

A dragon!

That?

A knight!

A royal
